sn3.5:1.2: As he was sitting to one side, King Pasenadi Kosala said to the Blessed One:
sn3.5:1.3: “Just now, lord, while I was alone in seclusion, this train of thought arose in my awareness:
sn3.5:1.4: ‘Who have themselves protected, and who leave themselves unprotected?’
sn3.5:1.6: ‘Those who engage in bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, & mental misconduct
sn3.5:1.8: Even though a squadron of elephant troops might protect them, a squadron of cavalry troops, a squadron of chariot troops, a squadron of infantry troops might protect them, still
sn3.5:1.11: Because that’s an external protection, not an internal one.
sn3.5:1.13: But those who engage in good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, & good mental conduct
sn3.5:1.15: Even though neither a squadron of elephant troops, a squadron of cavalry troops, a squadron of chariot troops, nor a squadron of infantry troops might protect them,
sn3.5:1.18: Because that’s an internal protection, not an external one.
sn3.5:2.1: “That’s the way it is, great king! That’s the way it is!
sn3.5:2.2: Those who engage in bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, & mental misconduct leave themselves unprotected. Even though a squadron of elephant troops might protect them, a squadron of cavalry troops, a squadron of chariot troops, a squadron of infantry troops might protect them, still
sn3.5:2.5: Because that’s an external protection, not an internal one.
sn3.5:2.7: But those who engage in good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, & good mental conduct
sn3.5:2.9: Even though neither a squadron of elephant troops, a squadron of cavalry troops, a squadron of chariot troops, nor a squadron of infantry troops might protect them, still
sn3.5:2.12: Because that’s an internal protection, not an external one.
sn3.5:2.14: That is what the Blessed One said. Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
sn3.5:3.3: Restraint with the heart is good,
sn3.5:3.6: one is said to be protected.”
